LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF HUSTON JACKSON'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO REQUEST TO PRODUCE DOCUMENTS, SETS ONE AND TWO; TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST ALL DEFENDANTS JOINTLY AND SEVERALLY IS GRANTED, IN PART. NO OPPOSITION FILED. DEFENDANTS SHALL PROVIDE VERIFIED RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S SPECIALLY PREPARED INTERROGATORIES & REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCOUMENTS SETS 1 & 2 TO PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL BY THE CLOSE OF BUSINESS ON MAY 24, 2013. OBJECTIONS ARE WAIVED. MONETARY SANCTIONS OF $ 1,500.00 ARE AWARDED IN FAVOR OF PLAINTIFF AND AGAINST DEFENDANTS TO BE PAID NO LATER THAN JUNE 24, 2013. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR EVIDENTIARY AND ISSUE SANCTIONS ARE DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. MASTER COURT CALENDAR SET FOR MAY-28-2013 CONTINUED TO MASTER CALENDAR COURT ON AUG-12-2013 AT 9:30 AM IN 206. WILLIAM HOPKINS APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. RONALD CARTER SPECIALLY APPEARING FOR DEFENDANTS. MR. CARTER REQUESTED A CONTINUANCE OF THE TRIAL DATE FOR THE REASON THAT HE WILL BE SUBSTITUTING IN AS NEW COUNSEL FOR THE DEFENDANTS. THE COURT FINDS GOOD CAUSE TO CONTINUE THE TRIAL DATE FOR NEW COUNSEL COMING IN TO THE CASE AND FOR FAILURE OF DISCOVERY BEING RESOLVED. THE TRIAL DATE OF 5/28/13 IS CONTINUED TO 8/12/13 AT 9:30AM IN DEPT. 206. THE NEW TRIAL DATE WILL BE DEEMED TO BE THE ORIGINAL TRIAL DATE FOR STATUTORY CUT-OFF PURPOSES. PLAINTIFF WAIVES JURY TRIAL. THE SUBSTITUTION OF ATTORNEY SHOULD BE FILED TODAY AND A COURTESY COPY TO BE DELIVERED TO DEPT. 206. DEFENDANT TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CYNTHIA M. LEE; CLERK: R. VELUZ; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)

Notice Of Motion To Enforce Terms Of A Settlement
Off Calendar Apr-16-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. BRUCE LANING, Tel No. (707) 254-5036 specially appearing for attorney-of-record William R. Hopkins on behalf of the Plaintiff;
Atty. RONALD W. CARTER, Tel No. (510) 465-6500 appearing on behalf of the Defendants.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to PLAINTIFF HUSTON JACKSON'S Motion To Enforce Terms Of A Settlement, Plaintiff's motions for relief from dismissal and to enforce terms of a settlement are denied. Both motions were previously denied by orders filed January 22, 2015 and, even assuming that plaintiff is permitted to re-file his motions after they were denied, plaintiff has not provided any grounds why those earlier orders were erroneous. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Denise Doucette, CSR #5963 (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion For Relief From Dismissal
Off Calendar Apr-16-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. BRUCE LANING, Tel No. (707) 254-5036 specially appearing for attorney-of-record William R. Hopkins on behalf of the Plaintiff;
Atty. RONALD W. CARTER, Tel No. (510) 465-6500 appearing on behalf of the Defendants.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to PLAINTIFF HUSTON JACKSON'S Motion For Relief From Dismissal, Plaintiff's motions for relief from dismissal and to enforce terms of a settlement are denied. Both motions were previously denied by orders filed January 22, 2015 and, even assuming that plaintiff is permitted to re-file his motions after they were denied, plaintiff has not provided any grounds why those earlier orders were erroneous. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Denise Doucette, CSR #5963 (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion For Enforcement Of Terms Of Settlement
Off Calendar Jan-22-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF HUSTON JACKSON'S Motion For Enforcement Of Terms Of Settlement is denied. Plaintiff admits that Defendant never signed the agreement. CCP 664.6 requires that all parties sign the settlement to be enforceable under that provision. See Sully-Miller Contracting Co. v. Gledson/Cashman Construction, Inc. (2002) 103 Cal.App.4th 30, 37. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: A. James Robertson, II; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/AJR)

Notice Of Motion For Relief From Dismissal
Off Calendar Jan-22-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF HUSTON JACKSON'S Motion For Relief From Dismissal is denied. First, on 12/22/14, the court continued this motion and ordered Plaintiff to provide a courtesy copy of the moving papers in compliance with SF Local Rule 2.6B. The court warned Plaintiff that his failure to provide the documents would result in the motion being denied. Plaintiff failed to provide the papers. Second, the dismissal was entered over a year ago and the motion is untimely. CCP 473(b) requires that a motion for relief "shall be made within a reasonable time, in no case exceeding six months, after the judgment, dismissal, order, or proceeding was taken." ORDER SIGNED. Judge: A. James Robertson, II; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/AJR)
